entities.name: RAFFINOSE FAMILY OLIGOSACCHARIDES
entities.description: Widespread plant oligosaccharides with beneficial and anti-nutritional properties in plants and humans

entities.name: PLANTS
entities.description: Biological organisms in which RFOs are widespread and provide stress tolerance

entities.name: HUMANS
entities.description: Biological organisms affected by the anti-nutritional and prebiotic properties of RFOs

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: External factors that influence the concentration of RFOs in plants

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: Genetic makeup of a plant influencing RFO concentrations

entities.name: HARVEST TIME
entities.description: The timing of crop collection affecting RFO concentrations

entities.name: ANTI-NUTRITIONAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: Characteristics of RFOs that can interfere with nutrient absorption or cause digestive issues

entities.name: FLATULENCE
entities.description: Gas production in the digestive tract caused by RFOs in humans and animals

entities.name: ANIMALS
entities.description: Non-human biological organisms affected by RFOs

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: Plants whose consumption is deterred by RFO-induced flatulence

entities.name: HEAT
entities.description: High temperature stress that RFOs help plants tolerate

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Water scarcity stress that RFOs help plants tolerate

entities.name: COLD
entities.description: Low temperature stress that RFOs help plants tolerate

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: High salt concentration stress that RFOs help plants tolerate

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to resist pathogens, imparted by RFOs

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION
entities.description: The process of seed sprouting regulated by RFOs

entities.name: VIGOR
entities.description: The strength and health of a plant, regulated by RFOs

entities.name: LONGEVITY
entities.description: The lifespan of seeds or plants, regulated by RFOs

entities.name: LARGE INTESTINE
entities.description: The part of the human digestive system where RFOs have beneficial effects

entities.name: PREBIOTIC POTENTIAL
entities.description: The ability of RFOs to promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria

entities.name: BENEFICIAL BACTERIA
entities.description: Gut microbes whose growth is promoted by RFOs

entities.name: PATHOGENS
entities.description: Harmful microbes reduced by the prebiotic effect of RFOs

entities.name: PUTREFACTIVE BACTERIA
entities.description: Bacteria in the colon reduced by the prebiotic effect of RFOs

entities.name: COLON
entities.description: The lower part of the large intestine where RFOs affect bacterial growth

entities.name: ANTI-ALLERGIC
entities.description: A biological function of RFOs in humans and animals

entities.name: ANTI-OBESITY
entities.description: A biological function of RFOs in humans and animals

entities.name: ANTI-DIABETIC
entities.description: A biological function of RFOs in humans and animals

entities.name: NON-ALCOHOLIC FATTY LIVER DISEASE
entities.description: A liver condition that RFOs help prevent

entities.name: CRYOPROTECTION
entities.description: Protection against freezing, a biological function of RFOs

entities.name: PLANT STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to withstand adverse conditions, aided by RFOs

entities.name: GRAIN LEGUMES
entities.description: The primary focus of the review regarding RFO composition and health roles
